recommended_action: Check for missing colons (:) or mismatched parentheses and quotes.
Ensure consistent use of 4 spaces per level; do not mix tabs and spaces.
Verify the variable name is spelled correctly and was defined before being used.
Ensure you are not mixing incompatible types (e.g. adding a string to an integer).
Validate that the data content is appropriate for the function (e.g. non-numeric strings in int()).
Check that the list index exists; remember that Python indexing starts at 0.
Verify the key exists in the dictionary or use the .get() method for a safe default.
Confirm the object actually has the method or attribute you are trying to call.
Use 'pip install' to install the missing package in your current environment.
Check the file path for typos and use absolute paths if the script moves.
Add a check to ensure the denominator is not zero before performing division.
Ensure your recursive function has a base case to prevent an infinite loop.
Check if the module is installed or if there is a circular dependency.

error_message: File too large: too many records or columns
recommended_action: Split the file into smaller chunks or increase the memory limit of the importing application.

error_message: Map the remaining required fields to proceed
recommended_action: Review the list of mandatory attributes and ensure all necessary columns are correctly mapped in the CSV.
Ensure the last line of your CSV file ends with a linefeed (\n) or carriage return (\r\n) character.
Find and strip non-printing or POSIX non-compliant characters from your data using a text editor like [Notepad++](url).
Verify that the AWS CLI or user has the correct IAM permissions for the S3 bucket or object.
Check for unescaped special characters in the request string or CSV data and validate syntax.
Check if the temporary storage directory exists and has sufficient write permissions.

error_message: The specified action can be called only once
recommended_action: Verify user permissions; some roles cannot be inherited through teams and must be assigned directly.
Fix the mapping in the data project or ensure the CSV file contains all expected columns defined in the schema.

error_message: Literal does not match format string (ORA-01861)
recommended_action: Check that the date or number format in the CSV matches the expected format string in the database.

error_message: CSV delimiter appearing in data column
recommended_action: Wrap data values containing commas in quotation marks or use a different delimiter like a pipe (|) or semicolon (;).
Save the file as UTF-8 encoding to ensure all special characters are supported and readable across systems.
Recreate the association between the external stage and the storage integration if the latter was recreated.

error_message: CSV format error: Column count mismatch
recommended_action: Check for stray commas in your data and ensure every row has the same number of delimiters as the header.

error_message: 404 Not Found: Resource does not exist
recommended_action: Verify the endpoint URL or object ID; check the [Google Cloud Status Dashboard](https://status.cloud.google.com) for service outages.
Ensure true/false columns contain only '1/0', 'True/False', or 'Yes/No' as defined by the [Salesforce Data Loader](https://help.salesforce.com) requirements.
Truncate text fields to stay within the character limits of the target database schema.
Increase the timeout settings on your load balancer or optimize the CSV processing logic to handle larger batches.
Add a descriptive first row to your CSV or specify 'no header' in your [Pandas read_csv](https://pandas.pydata.org) or import settings.

error_message: Data type mismatch: expected Integer found String
recommended_action: Audit the specified column for non-numeric characters, spaces, or currency symbols that prevent mathematical parsing.

error_message: Access Denied: S3 bucket policy restriction
recommended_action: Update the [AWS S3 Bucket Policy](https://aws.amazon.com) to allow the 'GetObject' or 'PutObject' action for your user.
Use a 'Trim' function or a [Python script](https://docs.python.org) to remove leading/trailing spaces that cause lookup failures.
Clear temporary system files or redirect the output stream to a volume with more available storage space.
Save the CSV as 'UTF-8 without BOM' to prevent mapping errors in systems like [Microsoft SQL Server](https://learn.microsoft.com).
Identify rows with empty fields and provide a default value or remove the row before importing into the [PostgreSQL database](https://www.postgresql.org).
Review parent-child relationships in your CSV to ensure no record references itself or creates an infinite loop.
Ensure your SSH key is added to the [GitHub Agent](https://docs.github.com) or server authorized_keys file.
Check the server-side logs for a stack trace; the issue is likely a crash in the [backend API](https://developer.mozilla.org) logic.
Remove symbols like #, $, or % from your CSV header row to comply with [BigQuery table naming](https://cloud.google.com) rules.

error_message: Invalid URI: The format could not be determined
recommended_action: Check that URLs in your data columns are properly prefixed with http:// or https://.

error_message: Locked record: record currently being edited
recommended_action: Retry the import after the target record is released by the current user or background process.
Verify that the database service is running and that the [firewall allows traffic](https://docs.aws.amazon.com) on that specific port.
Increase the system's ulimit or ensure your script explicitly closes file handles after processing each CSV.
Refresh your [OAuth 2.0 access token](https://auth0.com) or re-authenticate to obtain a new session.
Ensure your request includes a valid CSRF token in the headers to pass [OWASP security checks](https://cheatsheetseries.owasp.org).

error_message: Account locked due to multiple failed attempts
recommended_action: Wait for the lockout period to expire or reset your credentials via the [identity provider's portal](https://support.microsoft.com).
Renew the certificate for your domain using [Let's Encrypt](https://letsencrypt.org) or check your system clock settings.
Reduce the frequency of requests or request an [API quota increase](https://developers.google.com) from the provider.
Use a virtual environment or [Docker container](https://www.docker.com) to isolate specific library versions.
Verify the domain spelling or check if the record is correctly configured in your [Cloudflare DNS dashboard](https://dash.cloudflare.com).

error_message: Resource limit hit: Out of Memory (OOM)
recommended_action: Optimize your script to use generators instead of loading the full CSV into a [Pandas DataFrame](https://pandas.pydata.org).
Ensure the remote socket is still open before sending data; check for network stability or server-side timeouts.

error_message: File not found in expected directory
recommended_action: Use absolute paths instead of relative paths to ensure your [Python or Node.js script](https://nodejs.org) locates the CSV correctly.
Increase the grace period in your [AWS ECS](https://docs.aws.amazon.com) or Kubernetes liveness probe settings.
Manually resolve overlapping changes in [Git](https://docs.github.com) and commit the fixed version.
Run 'docker system prune' or expand the EBS volume via the [AWS Console](https://console.aws.amazon.com).
Allocate more memory to the container, as this usually indicates an [OOMKilled](https://kubernetes.io) event.
Update your [JSON Schema](https://json-schema.org) or CSV mapping to align with the latest API version definitions.
Update your integration to use the newer version specified in the [developer changelog](https://developer.mozilla.org).
Check the status of your [GitHub Actions](https://github.com) self-hosted runners or increase your concurrent job quota.

error_message: Invalid CORS policy: Origin not allowed
recommended_action: Add your domain to the 'Access-Control-Allow-Origin' header in your [server configuration](https://developer.mozilla.org).
Verify that the backend service behind the [Nginx proxy](https://docs.nginx.com) is actually running.
Change the folder ownership to the web server user (e.g., www-data) using [chown](https://linux.die.net).
Check your local firewall settings or use [Speedtest by Ookla](https://www.speedtest.net) to verify stable upload bandwidth.
Run 'chmod 400' on your key file to restrict access as required by [OpenSSH security standards](https://www.openssh.com).
Ensure your request header 'Accept' matches the response format provided by the [API documentation](https://developer.mozilla.org).
Verify you are using [Stripe Test Mode keys](https://stripe.com) when hitting the development endpoint instead of live keys.
Implement a retry loop with jitter in your application logic to handle [SQL Server deadlock](https://learn.microsoft.com) exceptions.
Locate the cell referencing itself and break the link using [Excel's Error Checking](https://support.microsoft.com) tool.
Download and install the [Microsoft Visual C++ Redistributable](https://learn.microsoft.com) package.
Clear your [npm cache](https://docs.npmjs.com) or verify the integrity of the downloaded library.
Increase the 'client_max_body_size' in your [Nginx configuration](https://www.nginx.com) to allow larger CSV uploads.
Verify that the 'Secret' or 'Public Key' used to sign the token matches the one in your [Identity Provider](https://jwt.io).
Verify the tool is installed and added to your [System PATH](https://opensource.com).
Prepend 'sudo' to the command or use [chmod](https://linux.die.net) to grant execute permissions to the current user.
Check the bucket name for typos and ensure it is created in the correct [AWS Region](https://aws.amazon.com).
Grant the necessary administrative permissions in the [Salesforce Setup](https://help.salesforce.com) menu for the API user.
Delete unnecessary logs or temporary files in the [home directory](https://tldp.org) to free up allocated space.
Check for missing closing brackets, parentheses, or quotes in your [Python script](https://docs.python.org).
Generate a new credential in your [Developer Dashboard](https://developers.google.com) and update your environment variables.
Identify the process holding the port using [lsof -i :8080](https://linux.die.net) and terminate it.
Run 'pip install' or 'npm install' to fetch the missing dependency from the [package registry](https://www.npmjs.com).

error_message: Maximum execution time of 30 seconds exceeded
recommended_action: Increase the 'max_execution_time' setting in your [php.ini](https://www.php.net) configuration.

error_message: Partition column not found in data path
recommended_action: Ensure your CSV is placed in a directory structure that matches your [AWS Glue](https://docs.aws.amazon.com) or Spark partition keys.
Prompt the user to use a different payment method via the [Stripe Dashboard](https://stripe.com) or contact their bank.
Check the health of your DataNodes and adjust the replication factor in the [Hadoop HDFS config](https://hadoop.apache.org).
Redirect the customer to complete the [multi-factor authentication](https://www.checkout.com) flow required by their card issuer.

error_message: Too many columns for Parquet conversion
recommended_action: Flatten your data structure or remove unused fields before writing to [Apache Parquet](https://parquet.apache.org) format.
Review the transaction risk score and white-list the IP or email in your [PayPal/Braintree](https://developer.paypal.com) settings.
Increase the number of shards in your [Amazon Kinesis](https://aws.amazon.com) stream or implement producer-side aggregation.
Convert currency strings to [ISO 4217](https://www.iso.org) standard three-letter codes (e.g., USD, EUR).
Wait for the database engine to finish its [crash recovery](https://dev.mysql.com) process before attempting new writes.
Check connectivity to your [Kafka Broker](https://kafka.apache.org) and verify that the bootstrap servers are reachable.

error_message: CUDA out of memory: tried to allocate {x} MiB
recommended_action: Reduce the batch size in your [PyTorch](https://pytorch.org) training loop or use gradient accumulation.

error_message: Serial port not found / Permission denied
recommended_action: Verify the [Arduino](https://www.arduino.cc) or IoT device is plugged in and your user is in the 'dialout' group.
Re-export your model or update the [TensorFlow Serving](https://www.tensorflow.org) container to match the saved model format.
Check the physical antenna alignment or reduce interference for your [LoRaWAN](https://www.thethingsnetwork.org) or cellular IoT node.

error_message: Input contains NaN infinity or a value too large
recommended_action: Apply a [Scikit-learn SimpleImputer](https://scikit-learn.org) or drop rows with missing values before fitting the model.
Re-download the binary and verify the integrity before flashing the [ESP32](https://docs.espressif.com) or microcontroller.

error_message: Label mismatch: classes found in data do not match model
recommended_action: Audit your CSV labels to ensure they exactly match the [LabelEncoder](https://scikit-learn.org) classes used during training.

error_message: I2C device not responding at address 0x{xx}
recommended_action: Use an [I2C Scanner script](https://playground.arduino.cc) to verify the hardware address and check pull-up resistors.
Re-download the pre-trained weights from the [Hugging Face Hub](https://huggingface.co) and verify the file size.
Check the wiring for loose connections and ensure the [sensor library](https://learn.adafruit.com) matches the hardware version.

error_message: Docker: No space left on device
recommended_action: Run 'docker system prune -a' to remove unused images and containers or expand the [Docker Desktop](https://docs.docker.com) disk limit.

error_message: Hydration failed: Text content did not match
recommended_action: Ensure your [Next.js](https://nextjs.org) component renders the same content on the server and client to avoid DOM mismatches.
Verify the image architecture matches your hardware (e.g., use --platform linux/amd64 for [M1/M2 Mac](https://docs.docker.com) compatibility).
Wrap client-side code in a 'useEffect' hook or check for 'typeof window' to avoid running browser-only code in [Node.js](https://nodejs.org).

error_message: Docker: Error response from daemon: conflict
recommended_action: Remove the existing container with 'docker rm -f {name}' before trying to reuse the same [container name](https://docs.docker.com).
Review the parent element's position and opacity; use [MDN's Stacking Context guide](https://developer.mozilla.org) to fix layering.
Verify the build output path and ensure your [Web App Manifest](https://developer.mozilla.org) is correctly linked in the HTML head.
Run 'chmod +x entrypoint.sh' before building the [Docker image](https://docs.docker.com) to ensure the startup script can run.

error_message: Uncaught TypeError: Cannot read property of undefined
recommended_action: Use [Optional Chaining](https://developer.mozilla.org) (?.) to safely access nested object properties in your JavaScript/React code.
Compress images using [Squoosh](https://squoosh.app) or implement lazy loading to improve [Core Web Vitals](https://web.dev).
Run the application as Administrator or take ownership of the folder via [Windows File Explorer](https://support.microsoft.com).
Update your JSON/YAML definition to include all mandatory fields specified in your [Swagger/OpenAPI schema](https://swagger.io).

error_message: The service did not respond in a timely fashion
recommended_action: Check the [Windows Event Viewer](https://learn.microsoft.com) for service-specific timeouts and increase the ServicesPipeTimeout registry value.

error_message: Swagger: Discriminator value does not match any schema
recommended_action: Verify that the 'type' field in your payload matches the names defined in your [polymorphic API mapping](https://swagger.io).
Disable your antivirus email scanning temporarily or check your [Outlook account settings](https://support.microsoft.com) for port 587 (TLS).
Verify your SATA mode (AHCI vs IDE) in the [BIOS/UEFI settings](https://learn.microsoft.com) after a hardware change.
Run a link checker like [Broken Link Checker](https://www.npmjs.com) to identify and fix 404s in your Redoc or Docusaurus site.

error_message: The instruction at 0x{addr} referenced memory at 0x{addr}
recommended_action: Run the [System File Checker (sfc /scannow)](https://support.microsoft.com) to repair corrupted Windows system files.
Set the 'Content-Type' header to 'application/json' or 'text/csv' as expected by the [REST API endpoint](https://developer.mozilla.org).
Open the command prompt as an administrator and use [Regsvr32](https://learn.microsoft.com) to manually register the component.
Inspect container logs using [kubectl logs --previous](https://kubernetes.io) to identify application crashes or missing dependencies.
Verify the image name/tag and ensure [imagePullSecrets](https://kubernetes.io) are correctly configured for private registries.
Increase the memory limits in your [Pod manifest](https://kubernetes.io) or optimize the application's memory footprint.
Check for missing or misnamed [ConfigMaps or Secrets](https://kubernetes.io) referenced in your environment variables or volume mounts.
Check node resource pressure (Disk/Memory) and verify the [kubelet service](https://kubernetes.io) status on the affected worker node.
Scale your cluster nodes or reduce the [resource requests](https://kubernetes.io) of the pod to fit available capacity.

error_message: 0/1 nodes available: node(s) had taint {key: value}
recommended_action: Add a matching [toleration](https://kubernetes.io) to the Pod spec or remove the taint from the node.
Restart [CoreDNS pods](https://kubernetes.io) and verify the service name matches the internal cluster FQDN.
Ensure a [StorageClass](https://kubernetes.io) is defined or manually provision a PersistentVolume that matches the claim's requirements.
Change the [imagePullPolicy](https://kubernetes.io) to 'Always' or 'IfNotPresent' if the image is not already cached on the node.
Review your [Validating Admission Webhook](https://kubernetes.io) logs to identify policy violations (e.g., missing labels).

error_message: The connection to the server localhost:8080 was refused
recommended_action: Verify that your [kubeconfig](https://kubernetes.io) context is correct and that the API server is reachable.

error_message: User: {arn} is not authorized to perform: sts:AssumeRole
recommended_action: Update the [IAM Trust Policy](https://docs.aws.amazon.com) to allow the specific entity to assume the target role.
Check the physical write-protect switch on your drive or clear the 'read-only' attribute using [hdparm](https://linux.die.net).

error_message: Kernel panic - not syncing: VFS: Unable to mount root fs
recommended_action: Verify your [GRUB/bootloader configuration](https://www.gnu.org) and ensure the initramfs file exists for the current kernel version.
Configure the [CORS policy](https://developer.mozilla.org) on your server to allow the 'Origin' header from your frontend domain.

error_message: Inbound rule violation: Security Group blocks port {x}
recommended_action: Add an ingress rule to your [VPC Security Group](https://docs.aws.amazon.com) to allow traffic from the source IP range.
Increase the maximum number of open file descriptors in [/etc/security/limits.conf](https://linux.die.net) for the service user.

error_message: RBAC: user {u} cannot list resource {r} in namespace {n}
recommended_action: Create a [RoleBinding or ClusterRoleBinding](https://kubernetes.io) to grant the required 'list' verb for that resource.
Use [gdb (GNU Debugger)](https://www.sourceware.org) to inspect the stack trace and identify invalid memory access in your C/C++ code.

error_message: X.509: Certificate signed by unknown authority
recommended_action: Install the root CA certificate into your system's [trust store](https://manuals.gfi.com) or update the 'cacert' file.

error_message: EKS: Service account not found for pod
recommended_action: Ensure the [ServiceAccount](https://docs.aws.amazon.com) exists in the namespace and is correctly mapped to an IAM role.

error_message: Data loss: partial write to destination
recommended_action: Verify the [Snowflake COPY INTO](https://docs.snowflake.com) command options and check the 'ON_ERROR' parameter settings.

error_message: Deadlock found when trying to get lock; try restarting transaction
recommended_action: Implement a backoff and retry mechanism in your [MySQL](https://dev.mysql.com) or MariaDB application logic.

error_message: Spark: java.lang.OutOfMemoryError: GC overhead limit exceeded
recommended_action: Increase the 'spark.executor.memory' or optimize your [Apache Spark](https://spark.apache.org) transformations to reduce object creation.

error_message: Airflow: Task exited with return code Negsignal.SIGKILL
recommended_action: Check for memory spikes in your [Airflow Task](https://airflow.apache.org) and increase the container resources or worker memory.

error_message: Column '{name}' has incompatible types (Int64 vs String)
recommended_action: Use a [Schema Enforcement](https://delta.io) tool or cast the column types explicitly during the ETL merge process.

error_message: BigQuery: Quota exceeded for query bytes scanned
recommended_action: Optimize your SQL by selecting only necessary columns and using [Partitioned Tables](https://cloud.google.com) to reduce scan costs.
Reset the consumer offset to the 'earliest' or 'latest' available position in the [Kafka Topic](https://kafka.apache.org).

error_message: Failed to write to Parquet: schema mismatch in row group
recommended_action: Ensure all files in the directory share the same schema before using [PyArrow](https://arrow.apache.org) to read them as a single dataset.

error_message: Redshift: Assert failure in query execution
recommended_action: Check for complex joins on large tables without proper [Distribution Keys](https://docs.aws.amazon.com) and simplify the query.

error_message: Glue: Failed to infer schema from empty path
recommended_action: Verify that the [S3 Source Path](https://docs.aws.amazon.com) contains files and that the Glue Crawler has the correct permissions.
Check your endpoint logs for crashes; ensure your [Webhook Listener](https://stripe.com) returns a 2xx status code immediately.
Verify the selected [Conda environment](https://docs.conda.io) is still valid and that the 'ipykernel' package is installed.
Verify the shared secret key and ensure you are hashing the [raw request body](https://developer.github.com) rather than the parsed JSON.
Install the missing library in your notebook cell using [%pip install scipy](https://ipython.readthedocs.io).
Offload heavy processing to a [background worker/queue](https://redis.io) and respond to the webhook trigger instantly.

error_message: ValueError: Expected 2D array got 1D array instead
recommended_action: Reshape your feature vector using [.reshape(-1, 1)](https://scikit-learn.org) before passing it to a Scikit-learn model.
Re-register the webhook URL via the [API Dashboard](https://developers.facebook.com) as the subscription has been explicitly removed.
Add a valid SSL certificate to your endpoint or use the [Certifi library](https://pypi.org) to handle CA bundles in Python.
Clear all cell outputs or remove large [Plotly/Matplotlib](https://matplotlib.org) figures before saving and pushing to GitHub.
Implement [idempotency logic](https://stripe.com) to check the unique event ID before processing the data to avoid duplicate records.
Check your [Android Studio](https://developer.android.com) XML files for syntax errors or duplicate resource IDs.
Add the required privacy description in [Xcode](https://developer.apple.com) to prevent crashes on iOS 10+.
Verify your network connection or check the target's DNS records using [nslookup](https://learn.microsoft.com).

error_message: Metasploit: Exploit completed but no session was created
recommended_action: Disable the target's antivirus/firewall or verify the [LHOST/LPORT](https://docs.metasploit.com) settings match your listener.

error_message: Burp Suite: Proxy not responding on 127.0.0.1:8080
recommended_action: Check if another process is using the port or update the [Burp Proxy listener settings](https://portswigger.net).

error_message: Keystore file '{path}' not found for signing
recommended_action: Verify the 'signingConfigs' in your [Gradle build file](https://developer.android.com) point to a valid .jks or .keystore file.
Run 'pod repo update' and 'pod install' to sync your [iOS dependencies](https://guides.cocoapods.org) with the latest specs.

error_message: Hydra: Maximum number of connections reached
recommended_action: Reduce the number of parallel tasks (-t) to avoid getting blocked by the [target server's](https://github.com) rate-limiting policy.
Generate a new distribution certificate in the [Apple Developer Portal](https://developer.apple.com) and refresh your Xcode profiles.

error_message: SQLMap: Target URL appears to be not injectable
recommended_action: Try increasing the '--level' or '--risk' parameters or check for [WAF/IPS interference](https://sqlmap.org).

error_message: modprobe: ERROR: could not insert module: Operation not permitted
recommended_action: Check if [Secure Boot](https://wiki.ubuntu.com) is enabled in BIOS; unsigned third-party drivers are blocked by default.

error_message: Eni limit reached: cannot attach more interfaces
recommended_action: Upgrade your [AWS EC2 Instance Type](https://docs.aws.amazon.com) to one that supports more Elastic Network Interfaces.

error_message: insmod: error inserting 'module.ko': -1 Invalid module format
recommended_action: Ensure the driver was compiled against your current [Linux Kernel Version](https://www.kernel.org) using 'uname -r'.

error_message: Ingress: Controller not found for class {name}
recommended_action: Verify that your [Kubernetes IngressClass](https://kubernetes.io) matches the one defined in your NGINX or Traefik controller.

error_message: Stuck at 'Waiting for module to be loaded'
recommended_action: Check 'dmesg' for hardware conflicts or [IRQ mismatches](https://tldp.org) preventing the driver from initializing.

error_message: CNI: Failed to assign IP address (No IP addresses available)
recommended_action: Expand the [VPC Secondary CIDR](https://docs.aws.amazon.com) or clean up stale network interfaces in your Kubernetes cluster.
Reinstall the [NVIDIA Proprietary Driver](https://www.nvidia.com) and ensure the kernel headers are installed for your OS.
Check your [Load Balancer Target Group](https://docs.aws.amazon.com) health check path and port configuration.

error_message: Write error: No space left on device (on a non-full disk)
recommended_action: Check for [Inode Exhaustion](https://linux.die.net) using 'df -i'; delete numerous small files or session logs to free up space.
Update your [VPC Route Table](https://docs.aws.amazon.com) to point to an active Internet Gateway or NAT Gateway.

error_message: FATAL: remaining connection slots are reserved for non-replication superuser connections
recommended_action: Increase 'max_connections' in [postgresql.conf](https://www.postgresql.org) or implement a connection pooler like [PgBouncer](https://www.pgbouncer.org).

error_message: Pipeline failed: No space left on device (CI runner)
recommended_action: Clear the [Docker build cache](https://docs.docker.com) or increase the ephemeral storage of your [GitHub Actions](https://docs.github.com) or GitLab runner.

error_message: PostgreSQL: autovacuum not keeping up with bloat
recommended_action: Tune 'autovacuum_vacuum_scale_factor' and 'autovacuum_cost_limit' to trigger [vacuuming](https://www.postgresql.org) more frequently on high-write tables.

error_message: CI/CD: Script failed with exit code 127
recommended_action: Ensure the binary or tool (e.g., 'npm' or 'aws') is installed in the [CI container image](https://docs.gitlab.com) and added to the PATH.

error_message: MySQL: Error 1205 (HY000): Lock wait timeout exceeded
recommended_action: Optimize long-running queries or increase 'innodb_lock_wait_timeout' in the [MySQL configuration](https://dev.mysql.com).

error_message: GitLab: Job is stuck: No runner is online
recommended_action: Verify that the [GitLab Runner service](https://docs.gitlab.com) is started and the registration token matches the project settings.

error_message: Slow Query: Sequential scan on large table
recommended_action: Create a [B-tree Index](https://www.postgresql.org) on the columns used in the WHERE clause to avoid full table scans.

error_message: Terraform: Error acquiring the state lock
recommended_action: Force unlock the state using [terraform force-unlock](https://developer.hashicorp.com) if the previous process crashed or was interrupted.
Increase 'max_allowed_packet' in [my.cnf](https://dev.mysql.com) to handle large BLOBs or long-running imports.
Increase the [JENKINS_JAVA_OPTIONS](https://www.jenkins.io) to allocate more RAM to the JVM (e.g., -Xmx2g).
Check the indicated line for missing colons (:), unclosed parentheses, or misspelled Python keywords.
Ensure the line following a function, loop, or if-statement has exactly 4 spaces of indentation.

error_message: TabError: inconsistent use of tabs and spaces
recommended_action: Standardize your script by converting all tabs to 4 spaces using your editor settings or [Autopep8](https://pypi.org).
Check for typos in the variable name and ensure it is assigned a value before being called in the current scope.

error_message: TypeError: can only concatenate str (not 'int') to str
recommended_action: Wrap the numeric variable in [str()](https://docs.python.org) before joining it with other text strings.

error_message: ValueError: invalid literal for int() with base 10
recommended_action: Verify the string contains only numbers; use the [.isdigit()](https://docs.python.org) method to validate data before conversion.

error_message: IndexError: list index out of range
recommended_action: Check the list length using [len()](https://docs.python.org) and ensure the index is between 0 and length minus one.
Use the [.get()](https://docs.python.org) method to retrieve dictionary values, providing a default value if the key is missing.
Verify the object type and check for typos in the method name; use `dir(object)` to see valid attributes.
Install the missing package via terminal using [pip install](https://pip.pypa.io) and verify your virtual environment.

error_message: FileNotFoundError: [Errno 2] No such file or directory
recommended_action: Check the file path for typos and use [os.path.abspath()](https://docs.python.org) to confirm the script's looking in the right folder.
Add a logic check `if divisor != 0:` before performing the math operation to handle null or zero values.
Close the file in other programs like Excel or ensure your script has the necessary [OS permissions](https://docs.python.org) to write to the folder.
Specify the correct encoding (e.g., `encoding='latin1'`) in the [open()](https://docs.python.org) function when reading non-UTF8 files.
Check your recursive function for a valid base case to prevent infinite loops, or use [sys.setrecursionlimit](https://docs.python.org).
Process large datasets in chunks using the `chunksize` parameter in [Pandas](https://pandas.pydata.org).

error_message: UnboundLocalError: local variable referenced before assignment
recommended_action: Ensure the variable is defined within the local scope or use the `global` keyword if modifying a variable from the outer scope.
Wrap the `next()` call in a try-except block or provide a default value like `next(iterator, None)`.
Verify the API response or file content is valid JSON; check for empty strings or HTML error pages being returned.

error_message: OverflowError: integer too large to convert to float
recommended_action: Use the [Decimal module](https://docs.python.org) for high-precision math or handle extremely large numbers as strings.

error_message: IndexError: list index out of range
recommended_action: Verify the list length using [len()](https://docs.python.org) and ensure the index is between 0 and (length - 1).
Review the list of mandatory attributes and ensure the column name in your code matches the CSV header exactly (watch for hidden spaces).

error_message: FileNotFoundError: [Errno 2] No such file or directory
recommended_action: Check the file path for typos and use [os.path.abspath()](https://docs.python.org) to confirm the script is looking in the right folder.
Check for circular imports where two files try to import each other, or verify that 'X' actually exists in the [module's documentation](https://docs.python.org).
Ensure the function returning your data actually succeeded; add a check `if data is not None:` before accessing attributes.
Verify the delimiter (e.g., , vs ;) and ensure no rows have extra commas. Use `on_bad_lines='warn'` in [pandas.read_csv](https://pandas.pydata.org).

error_message: StopIteration: Raised by next() when iterator is exhausted
recommended_action: Wrap your iterator logic in a try-except block or provide a default value: `next(my_iterator, None)`.

error_message: OverflowError: Python int too large to convert to C int
recommended_action: Use the [decimal module](https://docs.python.org) or ensure you are not passing excessively large integers to a library expecting a standard integer.
Ensure the last line of your CSV file ends with a linefeed (\n) or carriage return (\r\n) character to prevent [pandas](https://pandas.pydata.org) from skipping the final row.
Find and strip non-printing or POSIX non-compliant characters from your data using the [re module](https://docs.python.org) to apply a regex filter.
Install the missing dependency via your terminal using [pip install numpy](https://pypi.org) and restart your IDE.

error_message: SyntaxError: EOL while scanning string literal
recommended_action: Check for unclosed quotes in your CSV processing script or ensure string values don't contain unescaped line breaks.
Verify that the path provided points to a folder and not a specific file when using functions like [os.listdir()](https://docs.python.org#os.listdir).
Check that the path provided to [open()](https://docs.python.org) points to a file and not a folder.
Ensure the downstream process or network socket is still open before attempting to write more data to it.
Check if the target server is running and that you are using the correct port and [IP address](https://docs.python.org).
Increase the timeout value in your request or check your network connectivity/firewall settings.

error_message: IndentationError: unindent does not match any outer indentation level
recommended_action: Check the spacing of your return or control flow statements; they must align exactly with the opening statement of the block.
Enable error checking in [numpy](https://numpy.org) using `np.seterr(all='raise')` to identify where invalid math is happening.
Ensure you are not performing long-running operations or nested yields inside a generator's `finally` block when it is being closed.

error_message: SystemError: parent module '' not loaded cannot perform relative import
recommended_action: Use absolute imports or ensure you are running your script as a module using the `-m` flag (e.g., `python -m my_package.my_script`).
Check your [generator function](https://docs.python.org) for a return statement that is unintentionally triggering an early exit.
Define the required method in your subclass or ensure you are not calling an [abstract base class](https://docs.python.org) directly.
Verify the encoding string passed to [codecs.open()](https://docs.python.org) matches standard IANA names (e.g., 'utf-8', 'cp1252').
Release any existing [memoryview](https://docs.python.org) or buffer objects before attempting to resize or close the underlying data structure.

error_message: EOFError: EOF when reading a line
recommended_action: Ensure the input stream is not empty or provide a default value when using [input()](https://docs.python.org) in non-interactive scripts.
Implement a retry mechanism or use [select.select()](https://docs.python.org) to wait until the non-blocking socket is ready for data.
Verify the process ID (PID) exists before calling [os.wait()](https://docs.python.org) or check if the process has already been reaped.
Check for server-side timeouts or firewall rules that might be killing idle connections in your [socket](https://docs.python.org) logic.
Use `os.makedirs(path, exist_ok=True)` or check if the destination file exists before attempting to [create a new one](https://docs.python.org).
Wrap system calls in a loop to restart them if a [signal handler](https://docs.python.org) interrupts the execution.
Ensure the path passed to [open()](https://docs.python.org) points to a specific filename and not a folder path.
Verify the PID is correct and that the process hasn't already terminated before sending a signal via [os.kill()](https://docs.python.org).

error_message: ReferenceError: weak object read after garbage collection
recommended_action: Check if the object being tracked by a [weakref](https://docs.python.org) is still in scope before attempting to access it.
Ensure all `return` statements are nested inside a `def` block; use `sys.exit()` if trying to end a script at the top level.
Move the `yield` statement inside a function definition to correctly create a [generator](https://docs.python.org).
Check for missing closing parentheses `)` or brackets `]` at the very end of your script file.
Check for variable/function name collisions (e.g., naming a variable `list` and then trying to call `list()`).

error_message: TypeError: missing X required positional arguments
recommended_action: Verify the function signature and ensure you are passing all mandatory arguments during the [function call](https://docs.python.org).

error_message: ValueError: too many values to unpack (expected X)
recommended_action: Ensure the number of variables on the left side of the assignment matches the length of the [iterable](https://docs.python.org).
Install or upgrade the [setuptools](https://pypi.org) package to resolve build-time dependency issues.

error_message: ImportError: libX.so: cannot open shared object file
recommended_action: Install the required system-level libraries (e.g., `apt-get install libx11-6`) that the Python module depends on.
Implement a `try...except KeyboardInterrupt:` block to [gracefully shut down](https://docs.python.org) resources when Ctrl+C is pressed.
Identify which part of the code is calling `sys.exit()` and review the exit code to determine the [nature of the failure](https://docs.python.org).

error_message: TabError: inconsistent use of tabs and spaces in indentation
recommended_action: Configure your editor to [show invisible characters](https://code.visualstudio.com) to find and replace stray tabs with spaces.
Debug the logic preceding the [assert statement](https://docs.python.org) to understand why the expected state was not met.
Use the [math.isfinite()](https://docs.python.org) function to check for NaN or Infinity values before performing complex math.
Ensure you are not trying to annotate complex expressions; only simple [variable names](https://docs.python.org) can be annotated.
Set the `PYTHONIOENCODING` environment variable to `utf-8` or use `.encode('utf-8')` before writing to a [restricted stream](https://docs.python.org).

error_message: RuntimeError: dict changed size during iteration
recommended_action: Create a list of keys using `list(my_dict.keys())` before looping if you need to [modify the dictionary](https://docs.python.org) while iterating.
Check for [shadowing](https://docs.python.org) (e.g., you named your local file `pandas.py`, which is being imported instead of the real library).

error_message: RuntimeError: Set changed size during iteration
recommended_action: Create a [list()](https://docs.python.org) of the set before looping to avoid errors while adding or removing items.
Review the [Python Deprecation Cycle](https://docs.python.org) and update your code to use the newer alternative specified.
Release any existing [memoryview](https://docs.python.org) or buffer objects before attempting to modify the underlying data.

error_message: ImportError: cannot import name 'X' from partially initialized module 'Y'
recommended_action: Check for [Circular Imports](https://docs.python.org) where two files try to import from each other simultaneously.
Use a [context manager](https://docs.python.org) (with open...) to ensure files are automatically closed after use.

error_message: FutureWarning: {method} is deprecated and will be removed in a future version
recommended_action: Update your code to the recommended [Pandas](https://pandas.pydata.org) or Numpy function to maintain compatibility.
Check your [f-string](https://docs.python.org) expressions for unclosed curly braces or syntax errors inside the expression.

error_message: TypeError: 'async for' requires an object with __aiter__ method
recommended_action: Ensure the object you are iterating over is an [Asynchronous Iterator](https://docs.python.org).

error_message: RuntimeError: cannot enter context manager more than once
recommended_action: Review your [with statement](https://docs.python.org) logic; some objects cannot be reused as context managers.

error_message: StopAsyncIteration: Raised by __anext__ when iterator is exhausted
recommended_action: Wrap your async loop logic in a try-except block or ensure the [async generator](https://docs.python.org) has data.
Verify that the path points to a folder and not a file when using functions like [os.listdir()](https://docs.python.org).
Check that the path provided to [open()](https://docs.python.org) points to a file and not a directory.
Verify the server state and check for network timeouts using the [socket library](https://docs.python.org).
Use `os.makedirs(path, exist_ok=True)` or delete the existing file before attempting to [re-create](https://docs.python.org) it.
Verify the Process ID (PID) is still active before sending signals using [os.kill()](https://docs.python.org).
Ensure the process hasn't already been reaped by [os.wait()](https://docs.python.org) or check your subprocess logic.
Handle cases where the receiving process closes the stream before the sender is finished; use [signal.SIG_IGN](https://docs.python.org).
Restart the system call manually if a [signal handler](https://docs.python.org) interrupts it during execution.
Ensure you are using **Python 3.7+**; for older versions, use `get_event_loop().run_until_complete()` as per [Asyncio docs](https://docs.python.org).
Avoid calling async functions after the loop has shut down; use [asyncio.run()](https://docs.python.org) for proper cleanup.

error_message: ValueError: signal only works in main thread
recommended_action: Move signal-handling logic to the [Main Thread](https://docs.python.org) as background threads cannot trap OS signals.

error_message: TypeError: Object of type {type} is not JSON serializable
recommended_action: Convert complex objects like Datetime or Sets into strings or lists before using [json.dumps()](https://docs.python.org).
Upgrade your [Jinja2](https://pypi.org) or MarkupSafe package to fix compatibility with newer Python versions.

error_message: SyntaxError: non-default argument follows default argument
recommended_action: Rearrange your [function signature](https://docs.python.org) so all mandatory arguments come before optional ones.

error_message: RuntimeError: This event loop is already running
recommended_action: Check for nested event loops; use [nest_asyncio](https://pypi.org) if you are running async code inside a Jupyter Notebook.
Ensure you are not catching the [GeneratorExit](https://docs.python.org) exception and continuing to yield values.

error_message: SystemError: error return without exception set
recommended_action: Check for issues in **C-extensions** or compiled modules; ensure they are returning proper error states to the [Python Interpreter](https://docs.python.org).
Reinstall or repair your [Python environment](https://pip.pypa.io) using `python -m ensurepip`.

error_message: TypeError: iteration over non-sequence of type {type}
recommended_action: Verify that the variable in your `for` loop is actually an [Iterable](https://docs.python.org) like a list or dictionary.

error_message: AssertionError: list indices must be integers or slices not str
recommended_action: Check if you are trying to access a **List** using a string key; verify if the variable should have been a **Dictionary**.
Check if another instance of your [Flask](https://flask.palletsprojects.com) or [FastAPI](https://fastapi.tiangolo.com) app is already running on the same port; kill the process or change the port.
Check for [Circular Imports](https://docs.python.org) where two files try to import each other, or ensure your local filename doesn't shadow a library name.

error_message: TypeError: 'async for' requires an object with __aiter__ method
recommended_action: Verify that the object you are iterating over is an [Asynchronous Iterator](https://docs.python.org) and not a standard list.
Ensure your [generator function](https://docs.python.org) does not contain a `return` statement that unintentionally triggers an exit in a `next()` call.
Upgrade your [Jinja2](https://pypi.org) or [MarkupSafe](https://pypi.org) package to resolve compatibility issues with newer Python versions.

error_message: SyntaxError: non-default argument follows default argument
recommended_action: Rearrange your [function signature](https://docs.python.org) so all mandatory arguments (without `=` values) come before the optional ones.

error_message: ValueError: signal only works in main thread
recommended_action: Move any [signal handling](https://docs.python.org) logic to the application's Main Thread, as background threads cannot trap OS signals.

error_message: RuntimeError: This event loop is already running
recommended_action: Check for nested event loops; if using Jupyter, install and use [nest_asyncio](https://pypi.org) to allow nested calls.

error_message: TypeError: Object of type {type} is not JSON serializable
recommended_action: Convert custom objects, [Datetime](https://docs.python.org) objects, or Sets into strings or lists before passing them to [json.dumps()](https://docs.python.org).
Implement a retry loop or use [select.select()](https://docs.python.org) to wait until the non-blocking socket is ready for data transfer.
Check for server-side timeouts or firewall rules killing idle connections in your [socket](https://docs.python.org) or database logic.
Ensure the path passed to [open()](https://docs.python.org) points to a specific file and not just a folder path.
Verify the Process ID (PID) is still active before sending signals like [SIGTERM](https://docs.python.org#module-signal) via [os.kill()](https://docs.python.org).

error_message: StopAsyncIteration: Raised by __anext__ when iterator is exhausted
recommended_action: Wrap your async loop in a try-except block or ensure the [async generator](https://docs.python.org) has sufficient data.
Verify that the path points to an actual folder when using directory-traversal functions like [os.listdir()](https://docs.python.org).
Ensure the child process hasn't already been reaped or terminated before calling [os.wait()](https://docs.python.org).
Handle cases where the receiving process closes the stream; check [subprocess.PIPE](https://docs.python.org) communication logic.
Use a [with statement](https://docs.python.org) to ensure file descriptors are automatically released after the block finishes.
Update your [Pandas](https://pandas.pydata.org) or [NumPy](https://numpy.org) code to the suggested modern method to maintain future compatibility.

error_message: ImportError: libX11.so.6: cannot open shared object file
recommended_action: Install missing system dependencies via [apt-get install](https://manpages.ubuntu.com) or verify your Docker container includes required GUI libraries.

error_message: RuntimeError: dictionary changed size during iteration
recommended_action: Iterate over a copy of the dictionary keys using `list(my_dict.keys())` if you need to [delete or add](https://docs.python.org) items while looping.
Verify that the `yield` keyword is placed within a `def` block to create a [Generator](https://docs.python.org).
Run the script using the `-m` flag (e.g., `python -m my_package.script`) to correctly handle [Relative Imports](https://docs.python.org).

error_message: AssertionError: list indices must be integers or slices not str
recommended_action: Check if you are accidentally treating a **List** like a **Dictionary**; verify the data structure using `type(my_var)`.
Process heavy files line-by-line or use the `chunksize` parameter in [Pandas read_csv](https://pandas.pydata.orgreference/api/pandas.read_csv.html) to lower RAM usage.
Check if the preceding function returned `None` due to a failure; add a [Null Check](https://docs.python.org) before starting the loop.
Catch this exception with a `try...except` block to [safely close](https://docs.python.org) database connections or log files when a user hits Ctrl+C.

error_message: OverflowError: integer too large to convert to float
recommended_action: Use the [decimal.Decimal](https://docs.python.org) class for high-precision math when dealing with numbers that exceed standard float limits.

error_message: SyntaxError: f-string: empty expression not allowed
recommended_action: Ensure your [f-string](https://docs.python.org) curly braces `{}` contain a valid variable or expression.
